steps.title: Use the vertical shift
steps.content: Subtracting $9$ from every $y$-value shifts the entire line of best fit down $9$ units. The slope stays the same, and the $y$-intercept becomes $9$ less.

steps.title: Adjust the intercept
steps.content: The drawn line for data set A has $y$-intercept about $54.57$. Therefore, the intercept for data set B is $$ 54.57-9=45.57 $$ The answer is A.
